channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Trump Abandons $1.776 Billion Anti-Weaponization Fund Amid Political Backlash
item.description: President Donald Trump faced a significant political setback when his administration decided to halt the $1.776 billion anti-weaponization fund, which was intended to compensate individuals who claime...

item.title: South Africa on track to meet 2030 climate emission targets
item.description: South Africa is showing promising progress towards meeting its climate goals, aiming to limit greenhouse gas emissions to 420 million tonnes of CO2-equivalent by 2030. Most of the country’s emissions ...

item.title: Mary Nolan: Kenny Chesney's Longtime Partner and Animal Lover
item.description: Mary Nolan has been Kenny Chesney's partner since 2012, though the couple has never publicly confirmed marriage. Both share a deep love for animals and have adopted rescue dogs together. Despite being...

item.title: Report: Trump Administration Proceeded With DC Park Lease Despite Internal Environmental and Legal Concerns
item.description: A report based on internal documents reveals that officials within the National Park Service raised repeated legal, environmental, and ethical concerns about a proposed plan to lease nearly 40 acres o...

item.title: Wall Street Journal Criticizes Trump’s Approach to Iran and Lebanon Ceasefire
item.description: The Wall Street Journal editorial board sharply criticized President Donald Trump's handling of Iran, accusing Tehran of manipulating him into safeguarding Hezbollah, despite ongoing violations of cea...

item.title: Metro Vancouver to implement Stage 3 water restrictions starting June 8, with review planned for later summer
item.description: Metro Vancouver is set to introduce Stage 3 water restrictions beginning June 8 as part of efforts to manage regional water supply under increasingly dry and warm conditions. Officials say the decisio...

item.title: Lagos strengthens airport measures to prevent Ebola entry
item.description: Lagos State dey put strong measures for Murtala Muhammed International Airport (MMIA) to prevent Ebola from entering Nigeria. Even though we never get any Ebola case for Naija, the State health offici...

item.title: Pentagon spokesperson faces criticism over office reclassification and transparency claims
item.description: A spokesperson for Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, Joel Valdez, faced significant backlash after posting on social media that the Department of Defense is 'the most transparent War Department in histo...

item.title: Former Partner of Trump Envoy Makes Unverified Claims About Melania Trump
item.description: Former Brazilian model Amanda Ungaro has publicly accused Melania Trump of having been involved with Jeffrey Epstein as an escort, claiming this is how she met Donald Trump. Ungaro made the statements...

item.title: Senate session sees only minority senators after Jinggoy Estrada’s arrest
item.description: On June 1, 2026, the Philippine Senate plenary session was marked by a notable absence of majority bloc senators following the arrest of Senator Jinggoy Estrada on graft and plunder charges. Only mino...

item.title: Rep. Tom Kean Jr. sponsors bill while still absent from Congress due to medical leave
item.description: Rep. Tom Kean Jr., a Republican representing a New Jersey district, has been absent from Capitol Hill for over 75 days due to an unspecified medical condition. Despite his absence, Kean has recently b...

item.title: Serena Williams set to return to professional tennis at Queen’s Club
item.description: Serena Williams, one of the most decorated athletes in tennis history, is reported to be making a return to professional competition, with her comeback scheduled at Queen’s Club. The announcement has ...

item.title: Niger Renegotiates Oil Deals With CNPC to Boost National Stake and Jobs
item.description: On May 18, 2026, Niger finally reach agreement with China National Petroleum Corporation (CNPC), dem ending wahala wey don dey for more than one year. Under the new memoranda, Niger go get 45% share f...

item.title: ACLU sues Trump administration over alleged mistreatment at Camp East Montana ICE facility
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) has filed a lawsuit against the Trump administration over the conditions at Camp East Montana, a large migrant detention center near El Paso, Texas. The facil...

item.title: President Ruto Apologises to Northern Kenya and Outlines Development Plans
item.description: President William Ruto has officially apologised to the people of Northern Kenya for decades of state marginalisation that left the region underdeveloped. Speaking during the 63rd Madaraka Day celebra...

item.title: Commentary Argues Trump Struggles to Achieve Broad Cultural Influence After Freedom 250 Event Collapses
item.description: A Raw Story article highlights an opinion piece by political commentator Paul Waldman examining the collapse of President Donald Trump's planned Freedom 250 Concert. According to the report, several p...

item.title: Alberta introduces patient-focused funding model for selected surgeries in public hospitals
item.description: Alberta has introduced a new surgery funding approach called patient-focused funding, which links hospital funding to the number and type of surgeries performed. Initially rolled out last month in a d...

item.title: Trump's Controversial Settlement Fund Faces Strong Opposition and Possible Termination
item.description: A GOP analyst has likened President Donald Trump's nearly $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund to a scene from Monty Python, highlighting its dwindling viability. The fund, created as part of a sett...

item.title: Fans Misinterpret BTS Jungkook’s Instagram Poem Post as Breakup Joke
item.description: BTS member Jungkook drew widespread attention online after sharing a recent Instagram post that unexpectedly sparked humorous speculation among fans. The post featured an excerpt from the poem “Fallin...

item.title: Hosts of Charlie Kirk Show express concerns over Pride Month and societal changes
item.description: On the first day of Pride Month, hosts of The Charlie Kirk Show, Andrew Kolvet and Blake Neff, expressed strong objections to Pride celebrations, linking LGBTQ visibility to concerns about fertility r...
